bio: Mahmoud Alavi served as Iran's Minister of Intelligence for both terms of [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) (2013–2021). A cleric with a military background, he was previously head of the Political and Ideological Organisation of the Iranian Army — a post to which [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) had appointed him directly. He had also served as a deputy Defense Minister. As Intelligence Minister he navigated the tension between Rouhani's détente agenda and the hardline security establishment, and managed the period of the JCPOA negotiations and their aftermath.
editorial_notes: Born 1954. Head of Army Political-Ideological Organisation before appointment. Intelligence Minister 2013–2021 under both Rouhani terms.
